UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user of the EduSpark platform, encompassing both students and instructors, storing their general profile information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication and as a unique identifier for external authentication.

name.description: The full name of the user.

bio.description: A short biography or description provided by the user.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

role.description: The role of the user on the platform.

applicationStatus.description: The status of a teacher's application, if applicable.

subjects.description: For subject teachers, a list of subjects they teach.

experience.description: For subject teachers, a summary of their teaching experience.

class.description: For students and class teachers, the class they are associated with (e.g., '8', '10').

section.description: For students and class teachers, the section of the class they are associated with (e.g., 'A', 'B').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a subject category used to organize and classify courses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

description.description: A brief description of the category.

Lecture.title: Lecture
Lecture.description: Represents an individual lecture or content unit within a chapter.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lecture entity.

lectureNumber.description: The sequential number of this lecture within its chapter.

type.description: The type of content for the lecture (e.g., 'video', 'text').

content.description: The content of the lecture (URL for video, text for text).

durationSeconds.description: The duration of the lecture in seconds.

Chapter.title: Chapter
Chapter.description: Represents a chapter within a course, containing a list of lectures.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Chapter entity.

order.description: The sequential order of this chapter within its course.

lectures.description: An array of lectures that belong to this chapter.

Course.title: Course
Course.description: Represents an educational course offered on the platform, containing details about its content and instructor.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Course entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the course content and objectives.

instructorId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the instructor who created this course. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Course)

instructorName.description: Denormalized name of the instructor for display purposes.

instructorAvatarUrl.description: Denormalized avatar URL of the instructor for display purposes.

thumbnailUrl.description: URL to the thumbnail image for the course.

price.description: The price of the course, if it is not free.

difficultyLevel.description: The designated difficulty level of the course (e.g., 'Beginner', 'Intermediate', 'Advanced').

categoryIds.description: References to Category entities that this course belongs to. (Relationship: Category N:N Course)

targetClass.description: The specific class this course is intended for (e.g., '8', '10').

chapters.description: An array of chapters that make up the course.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the course was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the course was last updated.

status.description: The current status of the course (e.g., 'Draft', 'Published', 'Archived').

Enrollment.title: Enrollment
Enrollment.description: Records a student's registration and overall progress for a specific course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Enrollment entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the student who enrolled. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Enrollment)

courseId.description: Reference to the Course the student is enrolled in. (Relationship: Course 1:N Enrollment)

enrollmentDate.description: Timestamp when the student enrolled in the course.

completionDate.description: Timestamp when the student completed the course.

status.description: The current status of the enrollment (e.g., 'InProgress', 'Completed', 'Dropped').

overallProgressPercentage.description: The calculated overall progress of the student within the course, from 0 to 100.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID (`userId`) is the Firebase Authentication UID. Authorization relies on `request.auth.uid` matching `userId` for write access. Public fields are readable by all. The 'id' field within the document must match the path's 'userId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user.

definition.description: Public collection of course categories. Readable by all, only administrators (determined by `role` in UserProfile) can create, update, or delete categories. The 'id' field within the document must match the path's 'categoryId'.

Stores courses with 'Published' or 'Archived' status. Readable by all authenticated users. Only the 'instructorId' specified in the document can update or delete the course. The 'instructorId' field is denormalized from the creator's UserProfile for authorization independence. The 'id' field within the document must match the path's 'courseId'.

params.name: courseId
params.description: Unique identifier for the published/archived course.

definition.description: Stores courses in 'Draft' status, privately owned and editable by the instructor. The 'instructorId' in the path must match `request.auth.uid` and the document's 'instructorId' field for read/write access. The 'id' field within the document must match the path's 'courseId'. Documents in this collection must have 'status' as 'Draft'.

params.name: instructorId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the instructor.

params.name: courseId
params.description: Unique identifier for the draft course.

definition.description: Tracks a student's enrollment in a course. The document ID (`courseId`) represents the specific course enrollment for the `studentId`. Only the `studentId` can read/write their own enrollments, and `studentId` in the path must match `request.auth.uid`. Includes denormalized `courseInstructorId` for instructors to query enrollments for their courses without a `get()` call. The 'id' field within the document should match the path's 'courseId'. The 'studentId' field in the document must match the path's 'studentId'.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the student.

params.name: courseId
params.description: Unique identifier for the course the student is enrolled in (used as the enrollment document ID).

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for EduSpark prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization rules by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The foundational strategy is **Authorization Independence via Denormalization** to eliminate `get()` calls in security rules, ensuring atomic operations and simplifying rule logic.

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **User Profiles and Roles:** User profiles (`/userProfiles/{userId}`) are directly path-based, meaning `request.auth.uid` directly identifies the owner for write operations. Global roles, such as administrators, are determined by the `role` field within the UserProfile document. This requires a `get()` call in security rules to check for admin privileges, which is an acceptable trade-off for role-based access control on non-list operations.
2.  **Course Ownership & Status Segregation:** To handle different access patterns for course states, courses are segregated structurally. 'Published' or 'Archived' courses reside in `/courses/{courseId}`, where the `instructorId` is denormalized directly into the course document. This enables rules to verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.instructorId` without `get()` calls. 'Draft' courses are kept private within `/users/{instructorId}/draftCourses/{courseId}`, leveraging path-based ownership (`request.auth.uid == instructorId`) and also denormalizing `instructorId` within the document for redundancy and clarity. This structural segregation simplifies rules by ensuring all documents in a given collection path share a homogeneous security posture.
3.  **Course Content (Chapters & Lectures):** Course content, defined as chapters and lectures, is embedded as an array of objects directly within the `Course` document. This simplifies the data model for the initial creation and editing phase, especially for draft courses, as it allows the entire course structure to be managed within a single document. This avoids the complexity of managing nested subcollections during the draft phase and simplifies the security rules, as access to chapters and lectures is inherently controlled by the rules on the parent `Course` document.
4.  **Student Enrollments and Progress:** Enrollments are designed using path-based ownership at `/users/{studentId}/enrollments/{courseId}`, where the `studentId` in the path matches `request.auth.uid`. Critically, the `courseId` is used as the document ID for the enrollment, allowing for efficient `exists(/users/$(request.auth.uid)/enrollments/$(courseId))` checks to verify if a student is enrolled in a particular course. When creating an enrollment, a rule will verify that the student's class matches the `targetClass` of the course, which requires a `get()` call but is acceptable on a write operation. Once enrolled, read access to the course content is granted based on this enrollment, avoiding repeated `get()` calls on read operations.

**QAPs (Query as a Privilege) Support:**
*   **Public Course Discovery:** The `/courses/{courseId}` collection allows for public `list` queries. Access to individual courses is then controlled at the read (`get`) level, ensuring students can only access courses for their class.
*   **Instructor's Drafts:** The `/users/{instructorId}/draftCourses/{courseId}` collection allows an instructor to `list` their own draft courses by querying the path corresponding to their `uid`.
*   **Student Enrollments:** The `/users/{studentId}/enrollments/{courseId}` collection allows a student to `list` their own enrollments efficiently. The denormalized `courseInstructorId` field within `Enrollment` documents allows instructors to query and `list` all enrollments for their specific courses across all students.
*   **Categories:** The `/categories/{categoryId}` collection is publicly readable, supporting `list` operations for all users.

By embedding chapters/lectures and controlling enrollment, the security rules remain simple and performant for read operations, while ensuring students can only access content appropriate for their class.
